stories.headline: Sara Duterte's defense opposes prosecution bid to open sealed BIR box of tax records
stories.summary: Vice President Sara Duterte's defense team said on June 26 it filed a comment with the Senate impeachment court opposing the House prosecution's motion to open, inventory and mark a sealed Bureau of Internal Revenue box said to contain tax records of Duterte and her family. The Senate secretary said unsealing requires both parties' agreement and that only the impeachment court can decide. The prosecution also sought rules on trial languages and witness disclosure.
stories.context: The five-day pretrial conference concluded June 25 without the box being opened. Duterte faces impeachment articles covering alleged misuse of confidential funds and other accusations.
stories.watch_for: The Senate impeachment court is expected to issue its pretrial order by June 29; the trial proper is set to begin July 6, 2026.

stories.headline: Sandiganbayan rules ex-Energy chief Cusi was adequately informed of Malampaya graft charges
stories.summary: The Sandiganbayan's Third Division ruled on June 26 that the Office of the Ombudsman sufficiently informed former Energy Secretary Alfonso Cusi and co-accused of the basis of a graft case tied to the Malampaya gas project, finding the bill of particulars adequately alleged injury and damage. Prosecutors allege the government lost about $177.4 million in the 2019 sale of Chevron's 45 percent Malampaya stake to UC Malampaya.
stories.context: The case stems from the Department of Energy's approval of the buyout of Chevron Philippines' Malampaya stake by UC Malampaya, a unit of Dennis Uy's Udenna Corp.
stories.watch_for: Cusi's arraignment was reset to July 10, 2026; the accused have until July 1 to seek reconsideration and prosecutors until July 6 to respond.

stories.headline: Trillanes files third cyber libel complaint, naming senators Cayetano and Marcoleta over bribery claims
stories.summary: Former senator Antonio Trillanes IV said he filed a cyber libel complaint with the Department of Justice on June 25 against 18 self-described former Marines and senators Alan Peter Cayetano and Rodante Marcoleta, over claims aired at a June 4 hearing that he received money to bribe International Criminal Court prosecutors. Trillanes denied the allegations and called them politically motivated.
stories.context: Trillanes said it was his third related cyber libel complaint; the disputed claims arose during a Senate inquiry into flood-control projects.

stories.headline: S&P cuts Philippine 2026 growth forecast to 4.1%, its sharpest downgrade in Asia-Pacific
stories.summary: Manila Bulletin reported that S&P Global Ratings lowered its 2026 Philippine GDP growth forecast to 4.1 percent from 5.8 percent, the largest cut among Asia-Pacific economies it tracks. S&P cited stalled infrastructure spending after fund-misuse issues, weaker domestic demand and fuel costs roughly 25 percent above pre-war levels, and projected 2026 headline inflation at 4.8 percent.
stories.context: The revision leaves the Philippines below the regional average and follows the government's own downgrade of its 2026 growth target to 3.5 to 4.5 percent.

stories.headline: Barclays expects Philippines to borrow another $2 billion abroad before end-2026
stories.summary: Manila Bulletin reported that Barclays economists expect the Philippine government to raise an additional $2 billion in offshore borrowing by year-end, even after Manila declared its 2026 foreign borrowing program complete. The Treasury has raised $5.25 billion through dollar bond sales this year. Barclays projected full-year 2026 growth of 3 percent, below the government's target, and a fiscal deficit of 5.9 percent of GDP.
stories.context: Barclays holds an underweight rating on Philippine sovereign credit, citing governance and external pressures.

stories.headline: Government proposes P7.2-trillion national budget for 2027
stories.summary: Manila Bulletin reported that the Marcos administration proposed a P7.2-trillion national budget for 2027, about P407 billion (6 percent) larger than the 2026 budget and equal to 21.7 percent of estimated GDP. Acting Budget Secretary Kim Robert de Leon said the plan prioritizes completing flagship infrastructure and human capital programs, and assumes 2027 growth of 5 to 6 percent, inflation of 4 to 5 percent and an exchange rate of P60 to P62 per dollar.
stories.watch_for: The proposed 2027 budget will be submitted to Congress for deliberation.

stories.headline: Phivolcs continues monitoring earthquake swarm off Pangasinan, with 287 tremors recorded
stories.summary: The Philippine Institute of Volcanology and Seismology said it had logged 287 earthquakes in a swarm off Bolinao, Pangasinan as of 7 a.m. on June 26, with magnitudes from 1.8 to 5.1 and depths of 2 to 47 kilometers. The swarm began on May 27 and only one quake was felt, at Intensity II. Phivolcs urged coastal residents to stay alert and prepared.
stories.context: An earthquake swarm is a cluster of quakes with no single dominant mainshock that can persist for weeks and may or may not precede a stronger event.

stories.headline: DepEd completes psychological support program for Tacloban school community after deadly shooting
stories.summary: The Department of Education said it concluded a three-day psychological intervention for the community of San Jose National High School in Tacloban City following the June 22 shooting that killed three students and injured about 20. Conducted with the Department of Health, mental health professionals and faith-based groups, the sessions used counseling, storytelling and art therapy. The two teenage suspects remain in Department of Social Welfare and Development facilities.

stories.headline: Magnitude 6.5 earthquake strikes off Davao Occidental; Phivolcs warns of aftershocks
stories.summary: GMA News reported that Phivolcs recorded a magnitude 6.5 earthquake at 7:34 p.m. on June 26, located about 66 kilometers southwest of Balut Island in Sarangani, Davao Occidental, at a depth of 45 kilometers. The agency said no major damage was expected but warned of aftershocks, and a magnitude 5.2 aftershock was later reported off Glan, Sarangani. Intensity V was felt in parts of Sarangani and South Cotabato.
stories.watch_for: Phivolcs said it was monitoring for further aftershocks in the area.

stories.headline: DepEd outlines tighter public-school security measures after Paranaque safety audit
stories.summary: GMA News reported that the Department of Education on June 26 announced stricter security measures for public schools after a safety audit led by Education Secretary Sonny Angara at Paranaque National High School. The measures include handheld metal detectors at entrances, updated emergency drills, expanded CCTV monitoring, confidential reporting channels for bullying, and closer coordination with police and local governments.
stories.context: The measures are part of a nationwide school-safety campaign launched after recent incidents of violence in schools, including the June 22 Tacloban shooting.

stories.headline: Philippine fertility rate falls to record-low 1.7 children per woman in 2025, PSA data show
stories.summary: GMA News, citing Philippine Statistics Authority data, reported that the country's total fertility rate among women aged 15 to 49 fell to a record-low 1.7 children per woman in 2025, down from 4.1 in 1993 and below the 2.1 replacement level. The data also showed teenage pregnancy declining from 10.1 percent in 2013 to 4.8 percent in 2025.
stories.context: A fertility rate below the replacement level means a population would eventually shrink without migration; analysts cited mixed economic implications.

stories.headline: PAGASA forecasts monsoon and low-pressure rains over much of the Philippines
stories.summary: In its 4 p.m. forecast on June 27, PAGASA said a low-pressure area about 965 kilometers east of northeastern Mindanao and the southwest monsoon would bring scattered rains and thunderstorms over much of the country. Moderate to heavy rains that could cause flash floods and landslides were possible over Eastern Visayas, Northern Mindanao, Caraga, the Davao region and western coastal areas, while Metro Manila and other western areas could see scattered showers.
stories.watch_for: PAGASA issues updated weather forecasts and any thunderstorm or rainfall advisories at regular intervals.

stories.headline: US and Iran trade strikes and accusations after Strait of Hormuz cargo ship attack
stories.summary: Al Jazeera reported that the United States carried out strikes on Iranian missile and drone storage sites and coastal radar after blaming Iran for a drone attack on the cargo ship Ever Lovely in the Strait of Hormuz, and that Iran's Revolutionary Guard said it struck US sites in the Gulf in response. Both sides accused the other of violating a June 17 memorandum of understanding. Bahrain reported an Iranian drone attack on its territory, with no casualties cited.
stories.context: The exchanges test a roughly week-old US-Iran understanding meant to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, a key oil shipping route that had been largely closed for months.

stories.headline: Russia says it downed 660 Ukrainian drones overnight in one of the war's largest strikes
stories.summary: Al Jazeera reported that Russia's Defense Ministry said it intercepted 660 Ukrainian drones overnight across 13 regions, including Moscow and annexed Crimea, in one of Kyiv's largest long-range attacks of the war. Ukraine's campaign targeting energy and explosives sites damaged facilities in the Tula region, and Crimea declared a state of emergency over fuel shortages and power cuts.
stories.context: Ukraine has increasingly targeted Russian fuel and energy infrastructure to disrupt supplies and war revenue.

stories.headline: Qualcomm to design China-specific data center AI chip to meet US export rules
stories.summary: Nikkei Asia reported that Qualcomm, which unveiled a data center chip lineup to challenge Nvidia in the AI processor market, is designing a China-specific variant that complies with US export restrictions. Chief Executive Cristiano Amon said the new chip aims to help ease a global memory shortage amid rising demand for AI infrastructure.
stories.context: Chipmakers continue to tailor products for the Chinese market to navigate tightening US export controls on advanced AI hardware.

stories.headline: Ebola outbreak in DR Congo surpasses 1,000 cases with 254 deaths
stories.summary: Al Jazeera reported that the Democratic Republic of the Congo's Ebola outbreak had passed 1,003 confirmed cases and 254 deaths, driven by the rare Bundibugyo strain, for which there is no approved vaccine or specific treatment. Officials said incomplete contact tracing suggests the true number of infections is higher and that the epidemic may not have peaked, with armed violence displacing thousands into crowded camps.
stories.context: The World Health Organization declared the regional outbreak a public health emergency of international concern on May 17, 2026.
